---
title: "Shared-catch simulation model"
format: html

# NOTE: keep maintenance notes HERE, in YAML comments, not in an HTML comment in
# the body -- pandoc passes <!-- --> straight through to the published page, so a
# body comment puts all of this in public page source.
#
# apps/stacks-explorer-embed.html is a SNAPSHOT. It is generated in the
# tnc001-belize-em repo by scratchpad/build_stacks_explorer.sh, from the source
# app explorer/stacks-app.html. Taken at commit a1e7f19 ("lovely widget"). To
# update it, rebuild there and re-copy -- do not edit it here.
#
# The <symbol id="tuna"> below is a second copy of that build's generated
# fragment (scratchpad/tuna_symbol.html). The app carries its own copy, but that
# one lives inside the iframe, and <use href="#tuna"> resolves by id within a
# single document -- it cannot reach across the frame boundary. The inline fish
# in the prose need this copy.
---

```{=html}
<svg aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" style="position:absolute;width:0;height:0;overflow:hidden">
<defs>
<symbol id="tuna" viewBox="0 0 1536 639" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
<g transform="translate(0,639) scale(0.1,-0.1)">
<path d="M10399 6356 c-562 -107 -1046 -252 -1539 -459 -176 -74 -550 -260 -720 -358 -472 -271 -965 -642 -1356 -1021 -72 -71 -134 -128 -137 -128 -4 0 -36 20 -73 45 -116 77 -288 138 -499 175 -60 11 -245 33 -410 50 -453 46 -617 80 -790 165 -166 82 -259 177 -346 355 -101 205 -191 322 -285 371 l-46 24 -40 -45 c-57 -62 -143 -196 -184 -284 -95 -206 -152 -431 -194 -760 -11 -88 -21 -161 -23 -162 -1 -1 -65 -14 -142 -28 -401 -77 -831 -209 -1210 -373 -527 -228 -1008 -510 -1755 -1029 -135 -94 -335 -233 -445 -309 -110 -76 -202 -140 -204 -142 -9 -6 33 -83 58 -106 14 -14 58 -45 96 -70 80 -51 129 -99 144 -140 21 -55 18 -57 -71 -57 -131 0 -195 -27 -220 -90 -13 -34 3 -59 76 -118 128 -103 235 -146 549 -217 331 -74 376 -93 702 -285 305 -179 390 -215 622 -264 169 -35 283 -45 678 -56 417 -12 782 -44 1078 -95 148 -25 318 -127 842 -506 289 -208 447 -311 595 -386 96 -48 111 -53 173 -53 l67 0 1 38 c6 197 -26 306 -156 527 -31 53 -71 127 -89 165 -32 67 -73 196 -65 204 2 2 110 11 239 20 439 32 1098 114 1526 192 300 54 272 55 394 -8 58 -29 240 -144 405 -254 472 -316 610 -394 826 -470 398 -139 773 -230 1139 -276 142 -18 728 -18 870 0 148 19 431 68 520 91 l75 19 -235 -5 c-386 -9 -843 18 -1149 68 -484 77 -861 236 -1113 469 -84 78 -174 191 -238 300 -42 71 -120 255 -120 282 0 12 11 13 67 6 86 -12 202 3 285 37 80 32 148 77 271 179 112 93 204 152 262 167 61 17 163 6 230 -25 31 -14 59 -26 61 -26 2 0 4 27 4 60 0 102 29 137 163 200 l88 41 47 -23 c37 -18 62 -23 117 -22 81 1 120 17 262 100 188 111 275 138 359 115 30 -8 40 -8 43 2 7 24 97 102 141 124 32 15 78 24 161 32 168 14 313 50 633 156 476 159 715 205 1055 205 l137 0 165 -168 c91 -92 323 -341 517 -554 405 -446 599 -648 785 -817 377 -342 697 -556 1034 -690 105 -41 242 -85 227 -72 -5 4 -37 29 -72 55 -125 95 -257 244 -375 426 -87 134 -197 342 -420 790 -219 441 -277 542 -448 777 -185 253 -221 350 -222 593 -1 167 15 271 68 436 140 443 455 918 1091 1649 258 297 335 387 330 392 -7 8 -235 -71 -352 -122 -159 -68 -271 -130 -424 -233 -308 -207 -568 -459 -1020 -987 -385 -450 -662 -739 -860 -899 l-85 -68 -140 6 c-957 43 -1276 119 -1634 388 -88 66 -138 62 -177 -15 -28 -55 -65 -87 -99 -87 -48 0 -91 18 -102 43 -20 43 -66 88 -117 114 -47 24 -65 27 -231 34 -99 4 -194 12 -210 17 -94 28 -166 91 -213 185 l-29 58 -13 -32 c-63 -152 -226 -159 -376 -17 -56 52 -79 86 -100 142 l-10 30 -23 -48 c-42 -86 -126 -120 -226 -91 -99 28 -174 116 -191 223 -4 23 -8 42 -11 42 -2 0 -25 -11 -51 -24 -104 -53 -225 -70 -317 -45 -22 6 -97 37 -167 70 -154 72 -244 94 -332 81 -34 -6 -62 -9 -63 -8 -1 1 20 56 46 122 124 314 300 576 545 811 443 426 1063 712 2011 928 216 50 697 135 1012 181 68 10 122 19 120 20 -9 10 -519 51 -732 59 -130 4 -153 2 -316 -29z"/>
<path d="M10490 2146 c0 -2 7 -7 16 -10 8 -3 12 -2 9 4 -6 10 -25 14 -25 6z"/>
</g></symbol>
</defs>
</svg>
```

```{=html}
<style>
  /* sized to the silhouette's own 2.404 aspect ratio */
  .fishicon { height: .78em; width: 1.88em; vertical-align: -.06em; }
  /* The app will not reflow below ~435px -- the controls and the stack panel sit
     side by side. Narrower than that it gets cut off, so let the frame keep its
     width and scroll the WRAPPER sideways instead. The page body must never be
     the thing that scrolls horizontally, and a scrollbar on the wrapper beats
     one nested inside the iframe (nested scroll is miserable on touch). */
  .explorer-wrap { overflow-x: auto; }
  /* .column-page gives ~990px; centre a narrower frame in it. Floor on the width:
     the stacks panel is responsive but bottoms out at ~779px, below which a
     horizontal scrollbar appears INSIDE the app. That happens once the app drops
     under ~779px, i.e. the frame under ~808px (frame minus the body's 2 x .9rem
     padding). 840px keeps the app at ~811px — a slimmer window with ~30px of
     clearance above that floor, verified scroller-free across M and re-simulate.
     The height is only a FALLBACK for when the sizing script below cannot run.
     The app's real height is not fixed: the stacks grow with M (614px at M=12,
     785px at M=25 measured), and taller again once the controls wrap. */
  .explorer-frame {
    width: 100%; min-width: 460px; max-width: 840px; margin: 0 auto;
    height: 900px; border: 0; display: block;
  }
</style>
```

Paired counts that are both correlated and overdispersed are hard to model well. So, we built a model for exactly that: a negative-binomial trivariate reduction, reparameterised so its parameters represent quantities of interest that are intuitive and meaningful. See how it works by adjusting the dials in the interactive panel below.

Each longline set has a logbook (LB) count and an electronic monitoring (EM) count. Each count comprises fish seen by both methods (A = <svg class="fishicon" viewBox="0 0 1536 639" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#tuna" fill="#9C8B78"/></svg>) plus fish seen only by that method (LB-only = <svg class="fishicon" viewBox="0 0 1536 639" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#tuna" fill="#C4622D"/></svg>, EM-only = <svg class="fishicon" viewBox="0 0 1536 639" aria-hidden="true"><use href="#tuna" fill="#2D6A7F"/></svg>). M is the mean count, and congruence f is the fraction of it that is shared. The box on the left shows the model parameters; the ten stacks are sets randomly generated from the model. Non-zero bias β distributes the unshared fish unevenly between the two methods, without changing M. With κ = 0 each part of the count is generated by a Poisson distribution; with κ > 0, by a negative binomial (higher variance).

```{=html}
<div class="column-page explorer-wrap">
<iframe src="apps/stacks-explorer-embed.html" class="explorer-frame"
        title="Shared-catch simulation model"></iframe>
</div>
<script>
  // Match the frame to the app. The app is same-origin, so we can just read its
  // height -- no postMessage handshake needed.
  //
  // The height is NOT constant: the stacks grow with M (449px at M=1, 785px at
  // M=25), so a one-shot measure on load clips the app at high M.
  //
  // The obvious tool is a ResizeObserver on the app's body, and it is here -- but
  // it is NOT what this relies on. Measured in-page, the body's clientHeight
  // updates 629 -> 785 on an M change while the observer fires zero times: layout
  // runs, but the resize notification never gets delivered. So the load-bearing
  // mechanism is the event listeners. The app only ever changes height in
  // response to a slider or the re-simulate button, so listening for those on the
  // app's document catches every real case. The observer stays as a backstop for
  // anything the events miss (e.g. a viewport reflow).
  (function () {
    var f = document.querySelector('iframe.explorer-frame');
    if (!f) return;
    function fit() {
      var d = f.contentDocument;
      if (!d || !d.body) return;
      f.style.height = d.body.scrollHeight + 'px';
    }
    function attach() {
      var d = f.contentDocument;
      if (!d || !d.body) return;
      fit();
      // The app draws its SVG after load, settling ~4px shorter than the first
      // measurement. Re-fit a few times so the frame lands on the final height
      // rather than the one that existed for a frame and a half.
      [50, 250, 1000].forEach(function (t) { setTimeout(fit, t); });
      try { new ResizeObserver(fit).observe(d.body); } catch (e) {}
      // capture phase: fire even though the app stops these on its own handlers
      ['input', 'change', 'click'].forEach(function (ev) {
        d.addEventListener(ev, function () { setTimeout(fit, 0); }, true);
      });
      window.addEventListener('resize', fit);
    }
    f.addEventListener('load', attach);
    // may already be loaded by the time this runs -- 'load' would not refire
    try {
      if (f.contentDocument && f.contentDocument.readyState === 'complete') attach();
    } catch (e) {}
  })();
</script>
```

---

Built by Sea Through Science as part of a project to evaluate electronic monitoring of Belize-flagged longline vessels, and how this method compares with logbook data — a project run in collaboration with [JPEC Consulting](https://jpec.co.nz/){target="_blank"} for [The Nature Conservancy](https://www.nature.org){target="_blank"}.

Tuna silhouette: *Thunnus albacares* by T. Michael Keesey, [PhyloPic](https://www.phylopic.org/images/f4503c9f-35df-4a0d-a6ca-c66c87d68274){target="_blank"}, public domain.
